title: Making a good thing better: enhancing password/PIN-based user authentication with smartwatch

method.input_representation: 36 temporal features (Mean Strength, Standard Deviation, Average Deviation, Skewness, Kurtosis, RMS) across 3 axes for 2 sensors
method.training_strategy: Randomly selected 50% training / 50% testing set, randomized 10 times

evaluation.context: Achieved using Bagged Decision Trees on QWERTY keyboard (improved dataset)
Achieved using Bagged Decision Trees on QWERTY keyboard (improved dataset)

evaluation_protocol: Cross-validation with 10 random splits of 50% training and 50% testing data per user

reported_limitations: User must wear smartwatch on the hand used to type PINs
User must maintain consistent typing pattern
Not scalable if user has multiple passwords across websites

key_contribution: Demonstrated that smartwatch motion sensors can effectively differentiate users even when keystroke timings are imitated
Proposed a multi-factor approach combining traditional passwords with behavioral motion biometrics
main_strength: Resistant to sophisticated behavioral imitation attacks that defeat standard keystroke dynamics
main_weakness: Evaluation performed entirely in a laboratory setting
Dependent on the user wearing the device on the specific typing hand
insights.notes_for_review: The paper specifically addresses 'imitation attacks' using a specialized training interface (Mimesis), which is a more targeted attack than simple zero-effort imposters.
